prompt_text: Give me a healthy breakfast plan for a week for someone who skips mornings and has only 5 minutes before leaving.
response_text:
Given your time constraint, I've come up with a 5-minute healthy breakfast plan for a week. These ideas are quick, easy, and can be prepared in no more than 5 minutes:

Monday: **Yogurt Parfait**
- 1 cup Greek yogurt
- 1/2 cup mixed berries (fresh or frozen)
- 1 tablespoon granola
Combine all ingredients in a container and grab it on your way out. (Prep time: 2 minutes)

Tuesday: **Peanut Butter Banana Toast**
- 1 slice whole-grain bread
- 1/2 banana, sliced
- 1 teaspoon peanut butter
 Toast the bread, spread with peanut butter, and top with banana slices. (Prep time: 2 minutes)

Wednesday: **Overnight Oats**
- Prepare a jar or container with 1/2 cup rolled oats, 1/2 cup milk, and your choice of nuts or fruit the night before. In the morning, grab the jar and go. (Prep time: 1 minute in the morning)

Thursday: **Hard-Boiled Eggs and Fruit**
- Boil eggs the night before and keep them in the fridge.
- 1 hard-boiled egg
- 1 piece of fresh fruit (e.g., apple, orange)
 Grab the egg and fruit, and you're out the door. (Prep time: 1 minute)

Friday: **Avocado Toast**
- 1 slice whole-grain bread
- 1/2 avocado, mashed
 Toast the bread, mash the avocado, and spread it on the toast. (Prep time: 2 minutes)

Saturday: **Smoothie**
- Prepare a smoothie the night before or use a pre-made mix.
- 1 cup smoothie
 Grab the smoothie and go. (Prep time: 1 minute)

Sunday: **Muffin and Fresh Fruit**
- Prepare a batch of healthy muffins on the weekend.
- 1 muffin
- 1 piece of fresh fruit
 Grab the muffin and fruit, and you're ready to go. (Prep time: 1 minute)

These breakfast ideas are quick, nutritious, and can be prepared in no more than 5 minutes. You can also customize them to suit your dietary preferences and ingredient availability.
